channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Sweden beat Tunisia 5-1 in strong start to World Cup
item.description: Two goals from Ayari and one each from Isak, Gyokeres and Svanberg take Sweden to the top of Group F in Monterrey.

item.title: Uruguay squad lands in US after flight delay ahead of World Cup opener
item.description: Uruguay arrived in the US less than 24 hours before the kickoff time for their game against Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Lebanese remain sceptical despite US-Iran ceasefire announcement
item.description: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement has raised hopes in Lebanon.

item.title: Ivory Coast beats Ecuador 1-0 at FIFA World Cup with Amad Diallo goal
item.description: Ecuador looked strong before Diallo broke through in the 90th minute with a clinical left-footed shot to seal the win.

item.title: Ecuadorian World Cup fans takeover Philly’s Rocky Steps
item.description: Ecuadorian football fans packed Philadelphia’s Rocky Steps to cheer on the South American nation in its opening

item.title: US-Iran to sign a ‘peace deal’ on Friday: What we know so far
item.description: Trump says a US-Iran deal will be signed this Friday, as Tehran confirms plans to end hostilities.

item.title: Trump allies cheer Iran ‘peace deal’ announcement as Democrats seek clarity
item.description: US and Iran say memorandum of understanding to be signed on Friday, but no official terms yet released.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s climate migrants fear eviction as crackdown intensifies
item.description: Many settled in Zimbabwe’s fertile Eastern Highlands after drought left their home areas unable to support farming.

item.title: Iran’s Ghalenoei, Taremi decry US treatment before first World Cup game
item.description: Striker Mehdi Taremi says US travel policies are dampening the usual joy the football tournament ignites.

item.title: Stock markets soar, oil falls as US, Iran confirm deal to end war
item.description: Asian stock markets surge as Washington and Tehran announce agreement to end hostilities and reopen Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Claims Israel’s Beirut strike pushed Trump on Iran announcement
item.description: US diplomat Alan Eyre says despite the US-Iran ceasefire announcement, there is no deal until it has been formalised.

item.title: World Cup nations slam UEFA chief for ‘disappointing’ 48-team criticism
item.description: 'There's no such thing as an unimportant World Cup match,' 13 World Cup participant nations say in a statement.

item.title: BREAKING: US, Iran announce ceasefire agreement
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced a ceasefire agreement with Iran.

item.title: US-Iran ‘peace deal’ announced; Trump says Strait of Hormuz reopening
item.description: Trump announces that a ceasefire deal with Iran has been agreed, and that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen for shipping.

item.title: Japan deny Netherlands by fighting back twice in World Cup opener
item.description: Asia's most successful side, Japan, twice come from behind to draw 2-2 with Netherlands in Group F opener.

item.title: Lamine Yamal fit to start on bench for Spain vs Cape Verde at World Cup
item.description: ‌Victor Munoz is also fit to play, as coach Luis de la Fuente says he has no concerns over his players' fitness.

item.title: India beat Pakistan by 64 runs to open Women’s T20 World Cup campaign
item.description: Smriti Mandhana scored 68, and Deepti Sharma took five wickets as India thrashed Pakistan in front of a sellout crowd.

item.title: LIVE: Justin Gaethje upsets IIia Topuria to win title at UFC Freedom 250
item.description: Gaethje is the new undisputed UFC lightweight champion after Topuria retired at the end of round four at White House.

item.title: Gaza mourns four killed in Israeli strike
item.description: Cries of grief echoed through Gaza City for four Palestinians killed near the Jabalia refugee camp in northern Gaza.

item.title: Violence erupts during anti-G7 protests in Geneva on eve of summit
item.description: Violence erupts during anti-G7 protests in Geneva on eve of summit.

item.title: Swiss right-wing plan to cap population rejected by narrow margins
item.description: Swiss voters reject a proposed population cap that would cap the country’s population at 10 million.

item.title: Medical teams in Goma face mistrust from relatives of Ebola victims
item.description: Confirmed Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo have spiked up to 782.

item.title: Six people killed as helicopters collide over Brazil’s Rio de Janeiro
item.description: Two helicopters collided in midair before crashing into car park of electric vehicle dealership, fire service says.

item.title: Lewis Hamilton wins first F1 race with Ferrari in Barcelona
item.description: Seven-time world champion's maiden victory for the Italian manufacturer is also his first F1 win in nearly two years.

item.title: The president, the billionaires and the news
item.description: A media industry weakened by marauding billionaires is dealing with a US president eager to bend it further.
